optional_dependencies: TA-Lib (technical analysis library - requires C library)
version_and_installation.license: Apache 2.0 with Commons Clause (free for non-commercial use)

vectorbt_free.status: Maintenance mode - bug fixes and Python version support only
vectorbt_free.features: Core backtesting, portfolio simulation, indicators, signal generation
vectorbt_free.limitations: No new features being added to free version

vectorbt_pro.status: Active development with continuous new features
vectorbt_pro.access: Paid subscription with private GitHub repository access
vectorbt_pro.note: All research below applies to the free vectorbt version unless noted

vectorbt.base.description: Foundational utilities for array operations and pandas integration
submodules: array_wrapper - ArrayWrapper class for converting NumPy arrays back to pandas
column_grouper - ColumnGrouper for managing column groups
combine_fns - Functions for combining multiple arrays

ArrayWrapper.purpose: Stores pandas metadata and wraps NumPy arrays back to pandas format
key_methods: wrap() - Wrap NumPy array to pandas Series/DataFrame
ArrayWrapper.note: Immutable class - use Configured.replace() to modify

reshape_fns: soft_to_ndim() - Softly bring to specified dimensions
combine_fns: Functions for combining arrays with custom logic
Emphasis on stacking results for hyperparameter optimization
index_fns: Stack, combine, and cleanse MultiIndex levels
Operations on both index and columns

vectorbt.data.description: Data downloading, storage, updating, and management
submodules: custom - Custom data sources (YFData, BinanceData, etc.)

Data.description: Base class for storing and managing data as dict of Series/DataFrames
class_methods: download_symbol() - Override to implement custom download logic
from_data() - Create Data instance from existing data dict
align_columns(data, missing='raise') - Align data to same columns
align_index(data, missing='nan') - Align data to same index

YFData.description: Data class for Yahoo Finance via yfinance library
methods: download(symbols, start, end, **kwargs) - Download data from Yahoo Finance
warnings: Yahoo data may contain noise and missing points
Timezone handling: stocks in +0500, crypto in UTC

other_data_classes: CCXTData - CCXT library integration for multiple exchanges
SyntheticData - Generate synthetic data for testing

vectorbt.generic.description: Generic time series operations, applicable to any numeric data
submodules: ranges - Range record analysis (see separate section)

GenericAccessor.description: Generic accessor for time series operations
rolling_methods: rolling_apply(window, apply_func_nb, minp, on_matrix) - Apply custom function over rolling window
rolling_split(window_len, set_lens, left_to_right) - Split into rolling windows
groupby_methods: groupby_apply(apply_func_nb, on_matrix) - Apply function to groups
squeeze_grouped() - Squeeze groups into single elements
resampling_methods: resample_apply(freq, apply_func_nb) - Apply function over resampled periods
split_methods: split(splitter, **kwargs) - Split data using custom splitter
GenericAccessor.performance_note: Numba-compiled versions are 80-100x faster than pandas equivalents

StatsBuilderMixin.description: Mixin for building statistics from performance metrics
StatsBuilderMixin.method: stats(column, metrics, agg_func, group_by, settings, tags)
StatsBuilderMixin.usage: Preset statistics for signals, returns, portfolio components

vectorbt.indicators.description: Technical indicators - both built-in and from external libraries
submodules: factory - IndicatorFactory for creating custom indicators
built_in_indicators: FMAX, FMIN, FMEAN, FSTD - Fast statistics
RAND, RANDX, RANDNX - Random signal generators
RPROB, RPROBX, RPROBCX, RPROBNX - Probability-based generators
FIXLB, LEXLB, MEANLB, TRENDLB - Labeled variants

IndicatorFactory.description: Factory for creating custom technical indicators of any complexity
class_methods: from_talib(func_name, init_kwargs, **kwargs) - Build indicator from TA-Lib function
from_apply_func(apply_func, **params) - Build from custom apply function
initialization_parameters: class_name - Name for generated indicator class
input_names - List of input parameter names (e.g., ['close'])
advantages: Works with both Series and DataFrames
Caching for performance with large matrices
IndicatorFactory.external_library_support: 99% of indicators from TA-Lib, pandas-ta, and ta libraries

vectorbt.labels.description: Label generation for machine learning applications

submodules: telegram - Telegram bot integration for sending notifications

vectorbt.ohlcv_accessors.description: Specialized accessors for OHLCV (Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) data

vectorbt.portfolio.description: Core portfolio simulation and backtesting engine
submodules: enums - Enumeration types (DirectionT, OrderStatusT, TradesTypeT, etc.)

key_enums.SizeTypeT: Amount, Value, Percent, TargetAmount, TargetValue, TargetPercent

vectorbt.records.description: Sparse event data representation for orders, trades, positions, drawdowns
submodules: mapped_array - MappedArray for efficient record access

key_concepts.purpose: Store sparse event data without converting to matrix form

vectorbt.returns.description: Return analysis and risk metrics (similar to empyrical library)
submodules: metrics - Return and risk metric functions
qs_adapter - QuantStats adapter for additional metrics

ReturnsAccessor.description: Accessor for return series with financial metrics
construction: from_value(price, freq) - Convert price series to returns
rolling_versions: Note: All rolling versions are Numba-compiled for performance
properties: ann_factor - Get annualization factor based on frequency

integrations.empyrical: Numba-compiled versions of empyrical metrics with rolling variants

vectorbt.signals.description: Signal generation and manipulation for trading strategies

OHLCSTX.description: OHLC-based Stop Loss/Take Profit Exit Signals

OHLCSTCX.description: Chained OHLC Stop Signals - generates multiple entry/exit cycles

inputs: ts - Generic time series instead of OHLC

random_generators.RANDNX: Random signals with separate entry/exit n parameters

common_methods: plot() - Visualize signals on price charts

config.description: Nested configuration system with frozen keys

functions: is_tz_aware() - Check if datetime is timezone-aware

vectorbt.root_accessors.note: Provides vbt accessor on pandas objects

vectorbt._settings.structure: Nested configs for each sub-package, module, and class

portfolio_class_complete_api.description: Core class for portfolio simulation, analysis, and performance metrics

from_signals.description: Create portfolio from entry/exit signals with automatic signal management
from_signals.signature: Portfolio.from_signals(close, entries, exits, short_entries, short_exits, signal_func_nb, signal_args, size, size_type, price, fees, fixed_fees, slippage, min_size, max_size, size_granularity, reject_prob, lock_cash, allow_partial, raise_reject, log, accumulate, upon_long_conflict, upon_short_conflict, upon_dir_conflict, upon_opposite_entry, direction, val_price, open, high, low, sl_stop, sl_trail, tp_stop, stop_entry_price, stop_exit_price, upon_stop_exit, upon_stop_update, adjust_sl_func_nb, adjust_sl_args, adjust_tp_func_nb, adjust_tp_args, use_stops, init_cash, cash_sharing, call_seq, ffill_val_price, update_value, max_orders, max_logs, seed, group_by, broadcast_named_args, broadcast_kwargs, template_mapping, wrapper_kwargs, freq, attach_call_seq, **kwargs)

key_parameters.entries: Boolean array of long entry signals
key_parameters.exits: Boolean array of long exit signals
key_parameters.short_entries: Boolean array of short entry signals
key_parameters.short_exits: Boolean array of short exit signals
key_parameters.size_type: How to interpret size (Amount, Value, Percent, etc.)
key_parameters.call_seq: Custom call sequence for order execution

features: Prevents duplicate entries by default (use accumulate=True to allow)
Supports both long and short positions

from_orders.description: Create portfolio from predefined order specifications (most flexible and fastest)
from_orders.signature: Portfolio.from_orders(close, size, size_type, direction, price, fees, fixed_fees, slippage, min_size, max_size, size_granularity, reject_prob, lock_cash, allow_partial, raise_reject, log, val_price, init_cash, cash_sharing, call_seq, ffill_val_price, update_value, max_orders, max_logs, seed, group_by, broadcast_kwargs, wrapper_kwargs, freq, attach_call_seq, **kwargs)

key_parameters.size: Order sizes (broadcastable, default: None pulls from settings)
key_parameters.size_type: Amount, Value, Percent, TargetAmount, TargetValue, TargetPercent
key_parameters.max_orders: Maximum number of orders to allocate

from_order_func.description: Create portfolio using custom order generation functions (maximum flexibility)

key_parameters.flexible: Enable multiple orders per timestamp (bool, activates flex_simulate_nb)

features: Can generate multiple orders per bar (flexible=True)
Follows same broadcasting principles as from_orders()

from_random_signals.description: Generate portfolio from random entry/exit signals (for testing)
from_random_signals.use_case: Testing strategy robustness, Monte Carlo simulations

core_properties.close: Close price series used in simulation

risk_adjusted_metrics.deflated_sharpe_ratio: Haircut Sharpe with multiple testing penalty

stats_method.signature: stats(column, metrics, agg_func, group_by, settings, tags)
stats_method.description: Comprehensive performance analysis with customizable metrics

parameters.metrics: List of metrics to calculate (default: all)

configuration_features.group_by: Aggregate columns into portfolio groups (positions, names, NumPy array)
configuration_features.cash_sharing: Share capital across grouped columns (requires group_by)
configuration_features.ffill_val_price: Forward-fill NaN valuation prices (bool, default: True)
configuration_features.freq: Set time frequency for annualization ('D', 'H', etc.)

special_features.broadcasting: All inputs broadcast automatically across dimensions
special_features.numba_acceleration: Core simulation in compiled Numba for speed
special_features.logs: Detailed execution logs for debugging (when log=True)
special_features.flexible_sizing: Multiple size types and target-based sizing
special_features.stop_orders: Built-in stop-loss and take-profit in from_signals()

Orders.description: Captures information on filled orders from portfolio simulation

methods.plot: Visualize orders with price and buy/sell markers

available_metrics: Size statistics (min, max, average by side)
Fee analysis (total, min, max, average by side)

Trades.description: Base class for trade-like records (entry trades, exit trades, positions)

analysis_methods.profit_factor: Ratio of gross profit to gross loss
analysis_methods.sqn: System Quality Number for strategy evaluation

visualization_methods.plot: Generate trade visualization on price chart

EntryTrades.description: Entry trade records - trades from entry orders
EntryTrades.definition: Each entry order creates an entry trade record

ExitTrades.description: Exit trade records - trades from exit orders
ExitTrades.definition: Each order that closes/reduces a position creates an exit trade
ExitTrades.use_case: Analyze individual exit performance and timing

Positions.description: Position records - aggregated from sequences of entry/exit trades
Positions.definition: A position is created from a sequence of entry or exit trades
Positions.aggregation: get_positions_nb() fills position records by aggregating trades
Positions.use_case: High-level position analysis, full entry-to-exit lifecycle
Positions.note: Positions, entry trades, and exit trades produce same aggregated results

Drawdowns.description: Drawdown event records from time series

field_properties.peak_idx: Index of highest point before decline
field_properties.valley_idx: Index of lowest point in drawdown

active_drawdown_metrics.active_drawdown: Drawdown percentage of current active drawdown

methods.from_ts: Build drawdown records from time series
methods.plot: Visualize drawdowns with peaks, valleys, decline/recovery zones

Drawdowns.important_warning: Returns BOTH recovered AND active drawdowns - may skew results. Filter by 'recovered' attribute when needed.
visualization_features: Time series with marked peaks and valleys

Logs.purpose: Debug strategy logic and track state changes

vectorized_backtesting.core_principle: Process entire arrays/matrices at once instead of element-by-element iteration
advantages: Test thousands of parameter combinations in seconds
Numba JIT compilation for custom logic
vectorized_backtesting.example: Test 10,000 moving average window combinations across 5 assets simultaneously

broadcasting.description: Automatic expansion of arrays to compatible shapes for vectorized operations
features: No memory overhead with proper implementation
broadcasting.example: Single price array broadcasts with array of 100 parameter values

numba_acceleration.purpose: JIT compile Python functions to machine code for C-like performance
requirements: Functions must be Numba-compatible (limited Python features)
Cannot use pandas operations inside Numba functions
numba_acceleration.performance: Near C speed while maintaining Python readability

matrix_form.use_for: Price data, indicators, signals, continuous metrics

record_form.advantages: Memory efficient, fast event queries, automatic aggregation
record_form.classes: Orders, Trades, EntryTrades, ExitTrades, Positions, Drawdowns, Logs

workflow: 1. Extract NumPy array from pandas Series/DataFrame
3. Use ArrayWrapper to restore pandas index/columns/metadata
benefits: Speed of NumPy with convenience of pandas

approaches: 4. Chained signals for multiple entry/exit cycles
operations: Logic: AND, OR, XOR for combining signals

steps: 1. Define inputs: price data, signals/orders, parameters
2. Broadcasting: expand all inputs to compatible shapes
3. Simulation: Numba-compiled loop processes each bar
4. Order generation: from signals, predefined, or custom function
5. Order execution: size validation, fee calculation, cash management
6. State update: cash, positions, portfolio value
7. Record keeping: store orders, trades, logs
8. Post-processing: aggregate into trades, positions, drawdowns
9. Return Portfolio object with all records and metrics

features: Frozen keys (can modify values, not add/remove keys)
Preset metrics for common use cases

plotting_architecture.customization: Access via vbt.settings['plotting'] or method parameters

hyperparameter_optimization.example: Test all combinations of 100 windows × 50 thresholds × 10 assets = 50,000 strategies
hyperparameter_optimization.performance: Seconds to minutes (vs hours/days with traditional backtesting)
methods: Use stats() to find best combination
Filter by metrics, sort, select top performers
limitations: Memory constraints with very large grids

steps: 1. Download data: data = vbt.YFData.download('AAPL', start='2020-01-01')
2. Generate signals: fast_ma = vbt.MA.run(close, 10); slow_ma = vbt.MA.run(close, 50)
3. Create entries/exits: entries = fast_ma.ma_crossed_above(slow_ma); exits = fast_ma.ma_crossed_below(slow_ma)
4. Run simulation: pf = vbt.Portfolio.from_signals(close, entries, exits, fees=0.001, freq='D')
5. Analyze: pf.stats(); pf.plot().show()
1. Define parameter ranges: windows = np.arange(10, 100, 10)
2. Run indicator with all parameters: ma = vbt.MA.run(close, windows)
3. Generate signals for all combinations
4. Simulate all strategies: pf = vbt.Portfolio.from_signals(...)
5. Compare metrics: pf.sharpe_ratio().sort_values(ascending=False)
6. Select best: best_params = pf.sharpe_ratio().idxmax()
1. Define calculation function (can be Numba)
2. Create factory: MyInd = vbt.IndicatorFactory(class_name='MyInd', input_names=['close'], param_names=['window'], output_names=['value']).from_apply_func(my_func)
3. Run indicator: result = MyInd.run(close, window=[10, 20, 30])
4. Generate signals: entries = result.value_crossed_above(threshold)
5. Backtest as normal
1. Define Numba order function: @njit def order_func_nb(c, ...):
2. Implement logic: check conditions, calculate sizes, return orders
3. Optional: define pre_segment_func_nb for initialization
4. Run: pf = vbt.Portfolio.from_order_func(close, order_func_nb, ...)
5. Debug with logs: pf = ...from_order_func(..., log=True); pf.logs

approaches: 1. Periodic: Use from_order_func with segment callbacks
2. Threshold-based: Check allocations, rebalance when drift exceeds threshold
3. Cash sharing: Set cash_sharing=True, group_by for multi-asset portfolios

steps: 1. Split data: splits = data.vbt.rolling_split(window_len=252, set_lens=(1,1))
2. For each split: optimize on train, test on validation
3. Aggregate results across all windows
4. Analyze degradation from train to validation

speed_optimizations: Use Numba functions for custom logic
Minimize Python loops - use vectorized operations
Use flexible=False unless multiple orders needed
memory_optimizations: Use records for sparse data (orders, trades)

known_limitations: No multi-asset portfolio optimization in free version (PRO only)
Limited short selling controls (improve in PRO)
Free version in maintenance mode (PRO gets new features)
common_pitfalls: Forgetting to set freq parameter (affects annualization)
data_quality_warnings: Yahoo Finance: unstable, may have gaps/noise
Verify timezone handling (especially crypto vs stocks)

version_changes.note: Free version in maintenance mode - API stable
version_changes.python_support: Added Python 3.11, 3.12 support in recent versions

upgrading_to_pro.compatibility: PRO builds on free version - similar API with extensions
upgrading_to_pro.migration: Most free version code works in PRO with enhancements

repository_stats.status: Mature, stable (maintenance mode for free version)
